title: Piper aurantiacum
intro: Piper aurantiacum is a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of P. aurantiacum include piperine, piperic acid, and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Piper aurantiacum is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as tea and capsules, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Piper aurantiacum belongs to the family Piperaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pepper plants. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. Within the family Piperaceae, Piper aurantiacum is classified under the genus Piper, which comprises over 1,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Piper is further divided into various species, each with unique characteristics and habitats. Piper aurantiacum is specifically classified as Piper aurantiacum, with its specific classification still under research and confirmation by taxonomists.

side_effects: Piper aurantiacum is a plant species used in traditional medicine, but its use can lead to some adverse effects.
